visa_free.nationality: EU/EEA/Schengen member states (Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland)
visa_free.notes: EU/EEA/EFTA nationals and Schengen State residence permit holders do not need a visa. No day limit applies to EU citizens exercising freedom of movement.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required. Diplomatic/service passport holders require visa as of May 8, 2025.
90 days within any 180-day period. Hong Kong Special Administrative Region passport.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required.
90 days within any 180-day period. Macao Special Administrative Region passport.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required.
90 days within any 180-day period. Taiwan passport must include national identity card number.
90 days within any 180-day period. Biometric passport required.
90 days within any 180-day period. Also applies to British Overseas Citizens (BOC), British Overseas Territories Citizens (BOTC), British Protected Persons (BPP), and British Subjects (BS).

e_visa.nationality: Eligible third-country nationals as specified by Schengen regulations
e_visa.notes: Estonia participates in the Schengen short-stay visa system. Estonia does not operate a separate national e-visa portal distinct from the standard Schengen visa application. Schengen visa applications are submitted at Estonian embassies/consulates in person. No dedicated Estonia e-visa system confirmed on official sources.

visa_policy.notes: Estonia is a full Schengen member state (acceded 21 December 2007) and EU member state. Visa-free access for short stays (up to 90 days in any 180-day period) is governed by Schengen rules. The EU Entry/Exit System (EES) became fully operational on 10 April 2026, replacing passport stamps with digital biometric records for non-EU nationals entering the Schengen area; Estonia was the first Schengen state to begin operating EES on 12 October 2025. The ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is planned for launch in Q4 2026 and will require visa-exempt third-country nationals to obtain pre-travel authorisation (fee EUR 20) before visiting Schengen states including Estonia, but was NOT yet operational as of the research date (June 2026). Georgia ordinary passport (biometric) holders remain visa-free for 90 days/180-day period; Georgia diplomatic/service passport holders require a visa (bilateral suspension effective May 8, 2025; further reinforced by EU Commission Implementing Regulation effective March 6, 2026, valid until March 6, 2027). Vanuatu ordinary passport holders were removed from EU visa-free access effective December 2024 (EU Council Decision) due to investor-citizenship programme concerns - Vanuatu does NOT appear on the official vm.ee exempt list (last updated 18.12.2024) and is correctly NOT included in visa_free. Taiwan passport must include national identity card number.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen State residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Any third-country national holding a residence permit of a Schengen State does not need a visa to enter Estonia. Source page last updated 18.12.2024.

credential.subtype: Residence Card of a Family Member of an EEA National (Directive 2004/38/EC)

conditional_access.conditions: A family member of an EU/EEA/EFTA national who holds a residence card issued pursuant to Directive 2004/38/EC bearing the text 'Residence Card of a Family Member of an EEA National' may enter Estonia without a visa. Card must be valid.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Armenia. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Azerbaijan. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of biometric diplomatic passports of Belarus. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Belize. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Bolivia. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Cabo Verde. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Egypt. 90 days in any 180-day period.

Holders of diplomatic passports of India. 90 days in any 180-day period. Note: ordinary Indian passport holders require a Schengen visa; OCI cardholder status confers no separate entry benefit under this page.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Jordan. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Kazakhstan. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Kuwait. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Kyrgyzstan. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of biometric diplomatic passports of Mongolia. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Morocco. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of the People's Republic of China. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of the Philippines. 90 days in any 180-day period.

SUSPENDED. Georgian diplomatic and service passport holders require a Schengen visa. Initial suspension of the bilateral visa facilitation agreement took effect May 8, 2025 (per vm.ee official page). A further EU Commission Implementing Regulation under the revised Visa Suspension Mechanism entered into force March 6, 2026, valid until March 6, 2027 (source: EC press release IP_26_564).
SUSPENDED. Russian diplomatic passport exemption suspended per EU Council Decision (EU) 2022/333 of 25 February 2022.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Suriname. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Tajikistan. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Thailand. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Tunisia. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Turkmenistan. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic, special and service passports of Türkiye. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Uzbekistan. 90 days in any 180-day period.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Vietnam. 90 days in any 180-day period.

credential.subtype: Laissez-Passer issued by the United Nations

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of the Laissez-Passer issued by the United Nations are exempt from visa requirements for stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period.

cbi.notes: Estonia does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. Estonian citizenship is acquired through birth, restoration, or naturalisation after at least 8 years of legal residence (of which at least 5 years must be on a permanent residence basis). Estonian law generally does not allow dual citizenship for naturalised citizens. Source: https://www.politsei.ee/en/instructions/estonian-citizenship-for-an-adult

programs.name: Residence Permit for Major Investor
programs.notes: Requires a lasting direct investment of at least EUR 1,000,000 into an Estonian company or an investment fund primarily investing in Estonian companies. Income requirement: six times the subsistence level. Initial permit validity up to 5 years; extensions up to 10 years per renewal. The investment must not decrease during permit validity (except for market fluctuations). Separate path to permanent residence and citizenship follows standard naturalisation rules (8 years total residence, 5 years on permanent basis). Dual citizenship generally not permitted.

programs.name: Residence Permit for Start-up Entrepreneur
programs.notes: For founders/shareholders of early-stage companies with strong international growth potential. Must receive positive evaluation from Ministry of Interior expert committee (via Startup Estonia portal). Income requirement: four times the subsistence level annually. Initial permit up to 5 years; extensions up to 10 years. Not available to EU/EEA/EFTA nationals. Citizenship follows standard naturalisation (8 years total residence). Expert committee evaluation: 10 working days; permit decision: 90 days.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visa (D-Visa for Remote Workers)
programs.eligibility: Remote workers employed by companies registered outside Estonia, owners of companies registered abroad, or freelancers working primarily for foreign clients. Minimum monthly net income of EUR 4,500 (EUR 150/day benchmark). Must be able to work independently and remotely.
programs.notes: Long-stay D-Visa allowing remote workers to stay in Estonia for up to 1 year (365 days in 12 consecutive months). Application submitted at nearest Estonian Embassy. State fee EUR 120. A short-stay C-visa option covers up to 90 days. Distinct from e-Residency (which provides digital identity without physical stay rights). For full visa rules also see: https://vm.ee

programs.name: EU Blue Card (Highly Qualified Worker Residence Permit)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA nationals with a university/college diploma (3+ years programme) or 5 years of equivalent professional experience. Must have a valid employment contract with an Estonian employer paying at least 1.5x the average gross monthly salary (or 1.24x for shortage occupations). Employer must obtain permission from Estonian Unemployment Insurance Fund.
programs.processing_time: Up to 90 days for decision; residence permit card issued within 30 days after approval
programs.notes: Issued for duration of employment contract plus 3 months, maximum 27 months initially. Renewable for up to 4 years and 3 months per cycle. Excludes immigration quota for ICT sector and start-up employees. A2 Estonian language proficiency required after 5 years.

programs.name: e-Residency (Digital Identity Programme)
programs.eligibility: Open to entrepreneurs worldwide. Does not grant physical residency rights in Estonia.
programs.processing_time: Variable; card activated within 24 hours of pickup
programs.notes: Estonia's government-issued digital identity for global entrepreneurs. Provides remote access to Estonia's e-services and the ability to start and manage an EU company 100% online. Does NOT confer physical residency, citizenship, or the right to live in Estonia. Over 110,000 e-residents as of programme data. Launched 2014.

validation_notes: Re-validated 2026-06-27 against vm.ee (official visa-free list, last updated 18.12.2024). All ordinary-passport visa-free entries confirmed unchanged. Turkey/Türkiye confirmed correctly placed in conditional_access only (ordinary passports are NOT visa-free). CORRECTION: Georgia conditional_access diplomatic/service entry updated from 'visa_free (suspended)' to 'visa_required' with level field corrected, and notes updated to include the subsequent EU Commission Implementing Regulation of March 6, 2026 (IP_26_564) under the revised Visa Suspension Mechanism (valid until March 6, 2027). Russia conditional_access diplomatic entry level similarly corrected to 'visa_required'. No new countries added to or removed from ordinary-passport visa-free list since December 2024. Vanuatu confirmed still absent (correctly). ETIAS Q4 2026 timeline confirmed.

Re-validated June 2026 against vm.ee (official visa-free list, last updated 18.12.2024), politsei.ee (investor/startup permit pages), vm.ee long-stay D-visa page, and e-resident.gov.ee digital nomad visa page.

CORRECTION - ETIAS fee updated: Previous record did not specify the ETIAS fee. The fee was confirmed at EUR 20 (updated from the originally proposed EUR 7) per current official EU sources. The notes field in visa_policy now includes the EUR 20 figure.
CORRECTION - Taiwan notes clarified: Added that Taiwan passport must include national identity card number, matching the official vm.ee page language.

CONFIRMED - Vanuatu correctly absent: The EU fully revoked Vanuatu's visa-free access to Schengen (EU Council Decision, December 2024, effective from that date). Vanuatu does NOT appear on the vm.ee exempt list. The prior record correctly omitted Vanuatu. This validation confirms the omission is correct.

CONFIRMED - Visa-free list unchanged: The vm.ee official page (last updated 18.12.2024) lists the same set of ordinary-passport visa-free nationalities as the prior record. No new countries have been added or removed since the prior validation.

CONFIRMED - Digital Nomad Visa income threshold EUR 4,500/month: Verified on e-resident.gov.ee (EUR 150/day = EUR 4,500/month). The vm.ee long-stay D-visa financial-means table shows EUR 3,960/month (EUR 132/day) as the general telework minimum - this lower figure reflects minimum daily subsistence means for the visa application, while the program income benchmark is EUR 4,500/month. The prior record's EUR 4,500 figure is the correct program benchmark per e-resident.gov.ee.
CONFIRMED - Digital Nomad Visa fee EUR 120: Verified on vm.ee long-stay D-visa page.
CONFIRMED - Large Investor minimum EUR 1,000,000: Verified on politsei.ee.
CONFIRMED - ETIAS not yet operational as of June 2026: Confirmed planned Q4 2026 launch from multiple official/authoritative sources.

UPDATE 2026-06-29 - EES fully operational: The EU Entry/Exit System (EES) became fully operational on 10 April 2026 (source: European Commission, home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/news/entryexit-system-ees-fully-operational-2026-04-10_en). Estonia was the first Schengen state to begin EES operations on 12 October 2025. The visa_policy notes field has been updated to reflect this. EES does not change who is visa-free; it changes how entry/exit is recorded (digital biometric records replace passport stamps for non-EU nationals).

UPDATE 2026-06-29 - Visa-free list re-confirmed: Re-fetched vm.ee official page. The ordinary-passport visa-free country list (last updated 18.12.2024 on vm.ee) is confirmed unchanged. No countries added or removed since prior validation. All 62 entries in visa_free[] confirmed correct.
gaps: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System): Planned for Q4 2026 (October–December) but not yet operational as of research date (June 2026). When launched, visa-exempt third-country nationals visiting Estonia will need ETIAS pre-travel authorisation (EUR 20 fee). No confirmed specific launch date within Q4 2026; EU committed to give at least 6 months' advance notice.

The official vm.ee page (last updated 18.12.2024) has not been updated to reflect the March 6, 2026 EU Commission Implementing Regulation suspending Georgian diplomatic/service/official passport holders from Schengen visa-free travel. The conditional_access entry has been updated from both the vm.ee page (May 8, 2025 bilateral suspension) and the EC press release IP_26_564 (March 6, 2026 broader suspension, valid 12 months until March 6, 2027).
The official vm.ee page for diplomatic/official passport exemptions lists country groups rather than all individual countries; Russia's diplomatic exemption is suspended but the list may evolve.

Path-to-permanent-residence year requirements for investor and startup permits not explicitly stated on the official politsei.ee pages; standard naturalisation rules apply (8 years total, 5 on permanent basis) per citizenship page.
Exact minimum salary thresholds for EU Blue Card (in EUR amounts) are updated periodically by Statistics Estonia; exact current figures not re-verified from official page in this validation run.
No official separate e-visa portal (distinct from standard Schengen visa) confirmed for Estonia; Estonia uses standard Schengen visa application procedures.
Visa-on-arrival: Estonia does not operate a visa-on-arrival programme as a Schengen member state.

No official Estonian source found granting conditional entry to holders of non-Schengen foreign visas (e.g. US visa, UK visa, Canadian visa, Australian visa). Any such claims found only on aggregator sites (not recorded per official-source-first rule).
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card: No official Estonian or Schengen source found granting conditional entry to OCI cardholders as such. Not recorded per official-source-first rule.
Diplomatic passport bilateral agreement dates: The vm.ee page lists eligible passport types per country but does not publish the specific bilateral agreement dates for each country.
UN Laissez-Passer: Listed on vm.ee without specifying the applicable passport_types column; recorded under special_status with diplomatic as closest approximation.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (C Visa) – Tourism
visa_types.purpose: Permits tourism, leisure, and family visits to Estonia and the wider Schengen Area for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Standard adult fee EUR 90 (approx USD 97). Children 6–11 pay EUR 45; under 6 are free. Facilitation agreement countries pay EUR 35–EUR 67.50. Multiple-entry variants valid up to 5 years. Application must be submitted 15 days before travel and no earlier than 6 months ahead. Financial means required: EUR 70/day. Processing standard 15 days; complex cases up to 45 days.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (C Visa) – Business
visa_types.purpose: Permits business-related activities such as meetings, conferences, and negotiations in Estonia and the Schengen Area for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Same fee and processing as tourist C visa. Researchers, pupils, and students may be exempt from the fee. Multi-entry variants available for frequent business travellers.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (A Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Permits passage through the international transit zone of an Estonian (Schengen) airport between connecting flights, without entering the Schengen Area.
visa_types.notes: Required for certain nationalities transiting through Schengen airports. Holders remain in the international area and do not enter the Schengen territory. Fee EUR 90. Many nationalities are exempt from the airport transit visa requirement.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay D Visa – Short-Term Employment
visa_types.purpose: Allows a third-country national to enter Estonia to take up short-term paid employment with an Estonian employer for a period exceeding 90 days but not more than 365 days.
visa_types.notes: Fee EUR 120 (approx USD 130); children 6–11 EUR 60. Financial means requirement: EUR 1,320 one-time for short-term employment. Two consecutive D-visas cannot exceed 548 days within 730 consecutive days. Also allows up to 90 days travel in other Schengen states. Foreign documents require apostille/legalization and translation.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay D Visa – Seasonal Work
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to undertake seasonal agricultural or other seasonal work in Estonia for up to 365 days within a 12-month period.
visa_types.notes: Fee EUR 120. Financial means requirement: EUR 880 one-time for applicant; EUR 704 one-time for accompanying spouse. Separate from the standard work D-visa sub-category.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay D Visa – Studies
visa_types.purpose: Enables third-country nationals to enter Estonia for study at an accredited institution (university, college, or other recognised programme) for periods exceeding 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Fee EUR 120. Financial means: EUR 880/month for student; accompanying spouse requires EUR 2,100/month. PhD students have reduced spouse requirement of EUR 704/month. Schengen travel allowed up to 90 days per 180 days.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay D Visa – Digital Nomad (Remote Work)
visa_types.purpose: Allows remote workers employed by companies or clients outside Estonia to live and work in Estonia for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Fee EUR 120. Financial means threshold: EUR 132/day (approximately EUR 3,960/month) per vm.ee D-visa table; program income benchmark is EUR 4,500/month (EUR 150/day) per e-resident.gov.ee. Applicant must be employed by or own a company registered outside Estonia, or freelance primarily for foreign clients. Application submitted at nearest Estonian Embassy. Processing up to 30 days.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay D Visa – Start-up Business
visa_types.purpose: Permits founders of start-up companies with international growth potential to enter Estonia to establish and operate their business.
visa_types.notes: Fee EUR 120. Financial means: EUR 880/month for applicant; EUR 704/month for spouse. Typically a precursor to applying for Residence Permit for Start-up Entrepreneur. Expert committee evaluation required via Startup Estonia.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay D Visa – Working Holiday
visa_types.purpose: Allows young adults from partner countries to stay in Estonia for up to 12 months, combining tourism with incidental employment.
visa_types.notes: Fee EUR 120. Available to citizens of Australia and New Zealand (ages 18–35) and Canada (ages 18–35) and Japan (ages 18–30). Bilateral working holiday agreements underpin eligibility. Primary purpose is holiday/travel; work is permitted incidentally.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay D Visa – Family Reunification / Other Purposes
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals joining family members legally residing in Estonia, or other long-stay purposes not covered by other D-visa sub-categories.
visa_types.notes: Fee EUR 120. Financial means for 'other purposes': EUR 70/day for applicant and accompanying spouse; EUR 70/day per child. Covers family members of Estonian residents and other lawful long-stay purposes. Schengen travel up to 90/180 days also permitted.

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa – Diplomatic / Official Passport Holders
visa_types.purpose: Visa issued (or exemption granted) for holders of diplomatic, service, or official passports of countries with bilateral agreements with Estonia/EU, for official travel.
visa_types.notes: Approximately 25 countries' diplomatic/service/official passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days per 180-day period under bilateral agreements. Includes Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus (biometric), Belize, Bolivia, Cabo Verde, Egypt, India, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Mongolia (biometric), Morocco, China, Philippines, Suriname, Tajikistan, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkmenistan, Turkey, Uzbekistan, Vietnam. Russia's diplomatic exemption is suspended per EU Council Decision (EU) 2022/333 of 25 February 2022. Georgia's diplomatic/service exemption also suspended as of 8 May 2025.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay D Visa – Start-up Employment
visa_types.purpose: Allows a non-EU national to enter Estonia to take up employment at an officially recognised Estonian start-up company.
visa_types.notes: Fee EUR 120. Financial means: EUR 880 one-time for applicant; EUR 704 one-time for accompanying spouse. Intended for employees of start-up companies that have received a positive evaluation from the Ministry of Interior expert committee. Distinct from the start-up business D-visa (for founders).

outbound_official.notes: The Estonian Ministry of Foreign Affairs operates the 'Reisi Targalt' (Travel Smart) portal at reisitargalt.vm.ee, which is Estonia's official origin-side travel advisory covering visa requirements for Estonian ordinary-passport citizens traveling to 214+ destinations. Each country has its own dedicated sub-page (reisitargalt.vm.ee/sihtkoht/<country>/). There is no single consolidated downloadable list page; the portal is the authoritative per-country reference. Data below is drawn from individual country sub-pages fetched June 2026. Estonia is an EU/Schengen member, so Estonian citizens have full freedom of movement within the EU/Schengen area. Key electronic authorisation requirements: ESTA for USA, eTA for Canada, eVisitor for Australia, NZeTA for New Zealand. China visa-free is a temporary arrangement valid Nov 30 2024 – Dec 31 2026 (up to 30 days).

visa_free.name: China (temporary, until 31 Dec 2026, up to 30 days)

visa_on_arrival.name: Samoa (alternative to visa-free depending on purpose)

eta.name: United States (ESTA, mandatory for air/sea travel, USD 21, 2-year validity)
Canada (eTA, mandatory for air travel, CAD 7)
New Zealand (NZeTA, mandatory for air travel)
